summary: A 71-year-old man newly diagnosed with non-valvular atrial fibrillation (CHA2DS2-VASc 4: age, hypertension, diabetes, prior TIA) asks whether to start a DOAC (apixaban) versus warfarin. The held-out answer must reflect AHA/ACC/HRS 2024 AFib guideline preference for DOACs over warfarin in non-valvular AFib (except in mechanical valves and moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis), the major-trial-level evidence (RE-LY, ROCKET-AF, ARISTOTLE, ENGAGE-AF), the renal-dose adjustments, the role of CHA2DS2-VASc and HAS-BLED, and the differences in monitoring / interactions / reversal.

messages.content: I just got told I have atrial fibrillation. I'm 71, have high blood pressure and diabetes, and I had a small stroke (a 'TIA') two years ago. The cardiologist mentioned apixaban or warfarin. Which is better and which should I pick?

do_NOT: describe warfarin as preferred over DOACs in non-valvular AFib
skip the indication-exception (mechanical valves, moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis) where warfarin is still preferred
minimize the bleeding risk inherent to all anticoagulants
describe DOACs as having 'no monitoring needed' without the renal-function caveat

expected_sources_referenced: AHA/ACC/HRS/ACCP 2024 AFib guideline — Joglar et al., Circulation 2024
